one_time_description: Prior period adjustment - write-down of investment in Gwabalis Fisheries Society due to transfer of fishing licenses and quota to Gwabalis Fisheries Limited Partnership, reclassified from government partnership to portfolio investment at cost
economic_notes: First Nation has investments in Nanwakolas Carbon Credit LP (23%), Nanwakolas Offset LP (26%), Nanwakolas Timber LP (21%), Nanwakolas Business Corporation (17%), Gwabalis Fisheries Society (25%), Mamalilikulla Economic Development Corporation (100%), and Mamalilikulla Economic Development LP (100%). BC Timber Sales revenue of $164,000 and Indigenous Protected and Conserved Areas revenue of $139,478 received. Contribution to Coast Funds endowment of $75,000; endowment balance $1,110,165. Nanwakolas Carbon Credit LP dissolved during the year.
notes: Financial statements are for fiscal year ended March 31, 2023 (not 2024). Prior period restatement for Gwabalis Fisheries Society reclassification reduced 2022 investment, surplus and accumulated surplus by $990,146. Two wholly-owned subsidiaries (Mamalilikulla Economic Development Corporation and Mamalilikulla Economic Development LP) consolidated using modified equity method. Six partnership/enterprise investments also on modified equity. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $2,176,558 + Province of BC $279,110 + First Nation Health Authority $100,848 + BC Timber Sales $164,000 + IPCA $139,478 = $2,859,994 but IPCA and BC Timber Sales may be non-government; conservative total government transfers set to ISC + Province of BC + FNHA = $2,556,516. No schedule of remuneration disclosed. Advances from related parties (Mamalilikulla Forestry Corp and LP) of $2,080,592 are interest-free demand loans.
